summary.note: Refreshed 2026-05-30 from live GSC+Bing impression scan + fresh competitor recon. 5 location pages live (as Posts, pending conversion). Wave 2 below = winnable low-competition gaps: the geography moat (no competitor localises) + orphaned qualification-code pages (CKR1/HTR1/G3/WRAS/Legionella/LPG that nationals bundle but never give a dedicated page).

waves.scope: Live to date: 5 location pages + 20 blogs + 6 news + 1 topic + 6 GBP. Latest = Batch 4B (Maidenhead, ACS Reassessment Near Me, gas-engineers-heat-pump news, How to Become a Gas Engineer 2026).

WINNABLE-GAP WAVE (2026 fresh scan). Geography moat: no competitor localises course terms to the Bucks/Berks/Oxon corridor. Orphaned qualification codes: dedicated CKR1, HTR1, G3, WRAS, Legionella, LPG-changeover pages (nationals bundle, never give a standalone indexable page). Plus question/career content (ACS 'what if I fail', 'how to become a gas engineer', cost transparency) that Bing/GSC show real impressions for at positions 40-96. Full ranked list in docs/SEO_WAVE_PLAN_2026.md.

framework.strategy: Location-by-location content build, High Wycombe outward by tier
framework.weeklyCadence: Weekly target: 4 pages (1 service/blog, 1 location, 1 news, 1 course/business/partner authority page) plus 3 GBP posts. Friday rankings.

crawlerStatusCorrection.summary: SiteGround support confirmed normal public pages return HTTP 200 without x-robots-tag:noindex. Prior sg-captcha curl failures were from changing VPN/test IPs hitting SiteGround Antibot/WAF, not proof that Googlebot/Bingbot are globally blocked.
crawlerStatusCorrection.rule: Use GSC/Bing URL Inspection or SiteGround verified crawler logs for crawler-blocking claims. Do not use VPN/datacenter curl challenges alone.

contentGaps.note: No competitor localises: 'CCN1 course High Wycombe', 'ACS reassessment near me', 'gas training near Reading/Oxford'. Geo modifiers seen across GSC+Bing (Birmingham, Luton, Hampshire, Swansea, Dudley). Fastest defensible win.

CKR1, HTR1, G3, WRAS, Legionella, LPG changeover — nationals (Logic4/Able/Gastec) bundle these, almost none give a dedicated indexable page. Near-zero competition. Real Bing impressions for 'g3 course', 'g3 unvented qualification cost', 'do you need a g3 qualification'.

Biggest question-shaped Bing demand: 'what is an ACS course', 'what if I fail my ACS', 'how many papers in a reassessment', 'does the gas course include CCN1'. Perfect FAQ + blog fodder, currently landing on course pages at low positions.

Strongest Google demand (positions 44-96 = upside): 'how to become a gas engineer', 'becoming a gas engineer at 40', 'is gas a dying trade'. Logic4/VIVA rank generically; BTS wins on local + honest cost/timeline.
Heat pump skills shortage, Future Homes Standard 2028, MCS 2026 changes, low-temp heating design. Trending, link-worthy. MUST stay funding-neutral (no BUS/grant references per content rules).

waves.name: Wave 1 — Foundation (DONE)
waves.rationale: 5 location pages + 20 blogs + 6 news + 1 topic live (incl. Batch 4B). Migration debt: locations are Posts, no FAQs, Aylesbury URL fixed 2026-05-30.

waves.name: Wave 2 — Quick wins
waves.rationale: Orphaned qualification-code pages (CKR1, HTR1) + ACS eligibility FAQ/blog + code-explainer pillar. Cheapest, near-zero competition.

waves.name: Wave 3 — Local corridor
waves.rationale: Course+location pages across the Bucks/Berks/Oxon corridor — the geography moat. Built as Pages under a Training Near You hub.

waves.name: Wave 4 — Career + cost + decision
waves.rationale: Highest top-funnel demand, localised + honest cost. Career-change cluster already ranks 17-57 on Google.

waves.name: Wave 5 — News strand + heat-pump pathway
waves.rationale: Funding-neutral trend content. Heat-pump head terms closing; edge is local + qualification-pathway clarity.

competitors.name: Logic4training
weaknesses: Pivoting hard to electrical — gas/plumbing content thinning
All content is self-promotional, no neutral buyer guides
gapOpportunities: Neutral 'how to choose a training centre' content

competitors.name: Tom-Kat Training
strengths: Strong ACS reassessment eligibility detail (12-month/5-year rules)
weaknesses: Gas-only: no heat pump, water regs, Legionella standalone

competitors.name: Gastec Training
strengths: Milton Keynes — direct local rival (45 min)
gapOpportunities: 'CCN1 course High Wycombe/Bucks' (they only rank generically)

competitors.name: VIVA Training
strengths: Answers question-queries directly ('What is a CCN1 course', 'How long to be a gas engineer')
Fast on trends (heat pump apprenticeship Jan 2026)
weaknesses: Halifax/Yorkshire base — no southern local intent
gapOpportunities: Localised versions of their question content

keyGaps: STRUCTURAL MOAT: every serious competitor is national-generic — not one targets the Bucks/Berks/Oxon corridor locally for course terms.
Orphaned qualification codes (CKR1, HTR1, G3, WRAS, Legionella, LPG) get bundled by nationals, rarely a dedicated indexable page.
No competitor writes neutral buyer-guide content ('how to choose a gas training centre').
Heat pump window is closing — nationals now publishing heavily; BTS edge is local + qualification-pathway clarity (L3 ASHP + L3 low-temp design).
Funding-neutral news strand (skills shortage, Future Homes 2028, MCS 2026) is open — competitors lean on government-funding hooks BTS can't use.
